purpose: Phase 6 Network Map Engine for Matrix Reprogrammed. This data defines relationship maps that connect Power Atlas nodes, Evidence Vault lanes, books, and AI answers without turning association into accusation.
lineRules: Every line must say what it means.
A documented association is not proof of guilt.
A source lane is not a verdict.
Symbolic similarity remains commentary unless stronger evidence supports it.
Court records, declassified files, financial records, and datasets carry different evidential weight.
Map pages must route back to the Power Atlas and Evidence Vault.

maps.title: Intelligence Network Map
maps.subtitle: Agencies, liaison systems, oversight, declassified records, and surveillance infrastructure.
maps.summary: A structured map lane connecting CIA, NSA, Five Eyes, declassified archives, intelligence oversight, and the Intelligence Dossiers.
maps.riskBoundary: Do not treat institutional liaison, shared mission, or declassified record presence as proof of illegal conduct. Strong claims need source-specific evidence.

relationships.meaning: Released records can support historical claims when read with context and date boundaries.
Signals-intelligence relationships are public-record alliance structures, not automatic proof of abuse.
Oversight records define what was reviewed, contested, admitted, or limited by classification.

maps.title: Crime-State Overlap Map
maps.subtitle: Cartels, mafia systems, sanctions, ports, finance, corruption, and court records.
maps.summary: A map lane for organized crime as logistics, money, ports, underground banking, state penetration, violence, and public legal records.
maps.riskBoundary: Avoid operational detail that enables crime. Use court records, sanctions, regulator material, and credible reporting while separating allegation from adjudicated fact.

relationships.meaning: Sanctions and regulator records identify named targets and legal designations within their stated scope.
Legal filings and judgments must be separated into allegations, evidence, pleas, and findings.
Violence, exploitation, addiction, migration, and corruption are consequence lanes, not decorative statistics.

maps.title: War Machine Map
maps.subtitle: Governments, contractors, weapons firms, intelligence claims, procurement, sanctions, and human cost.
maps.summary: A map lane for the self-reinforcing system of war budgets, contractors, procurement, narratives, intelligence claims, and civilian consequences.
maps.riskBoundary: Contracts, lobbying, or influence are not proof of illegality. Separate procurement, policy advocacy, credible reporting, court records, and speculation.

relationships.meaning: Contracts and procurement show money flows within official records.
Contractor controversies must be grounded in legal records, official reports, and credible reporting.
Deaths, displacement, migration, and food stress must be sourced and dated.

maps.title: Elite Access Map
maps.subtitle: Conferences, finance, policy networks, documented association, and public/private influence.
maps.summary: A map lane for elite access systems such as meetings, conferences, asset managers, policy networks, and documented associations.
maps.riskBoundary: Attendance, contact, or association is not proof of command, control, guilt, or criminal intent. The line must stay labeled.

relationships.meaning: A verified attendance or contact line shows proximity, not wrongdoing.
Ownership exposure must be separated from operational control.
Soft power and agenda-setting need careful source boundaries.

maps.title: Symbolic Power Map
maps.subtitle: Freemasonry, ritual architecture, logos, patches, degrees, and interpretive boundaries.
maps.summary: A map lane for symbolic systems and esoteric commentary, connected to D.O.G, SYMBOL, Masonic books, and the evidence boundary that keeps symbolism from becoming unsupported accusation.
maps.riskBoundary: A symbol is not proof of conspiracy. Public records and symbolic interpretation must remain visibly separated.

relationships.meaning: Similarity can support interpretation, not proof of coordination.
Public membership claims require source checks and do not imply secret control.
Books route readers through interpretation and historical context.

maps.title: Medical Policy Power Map
maps.subtitle: Public health, compensation systems, adverse-event boundaries, pharma damages, and policy power.
maps.summary: A map lane for medical institutions, compensation records, adverse-event reporting, pharmaceutical power, causation boundaries, and human cost.
maps.riskBoundary: Adverse-event reports, compensation, settlements, proven causation, and official statistics are different categories and must not be merged.

relationships.meaning: A report is a signal, not proof of causation by itself.
Compensated claims must be separated from pending claims and reported events.
Public-health claims require dated datasets and clear categories.
